---
title: PFS - Spectral wave nesting
description: Add spectral output along a boundary line for a nested model
jupyter: python3
---

A common task in spectral wave modelling is setting up a nested model. The regional model needs to output spectral data along the boundary of the nested domain. This example shows how to programmatically add a spectral line output to a [MIKE 21 Spectral Waves](https://www.dhigroup.com/technologies/mikepoweredbydhi/mike-21-spectral-waves) setup file.

```{python}
import mikeio
```

## The regional model

Read the mesh to see the model domain.

```{python}
msh = mikeio.Mesh("../../data/north_sea_2.mesh")
msh.plot();
```

Read the setup file and inspect the existing outputs.

```{python}
pfs = mikeio.read_pfs("../../data/NorthSea.sw")
sw = pfs.FemEngineSW.SPECTRAL_WAVE_MODULE
outputs = sw.OUTPUTS

df = outputs.to_dataframe(prefix="OUTPUT_")
df[["title", "file_name", "type", "include"]]
```

The setup has 10 outputs, but none of them are spectral (`type=4`) line outputs suitable for nesting.

## Add a spectral boundary output

We'll add a spectral output along a line across the Thames Estuary, to provide boundary conditions for a nested local model.

Start by copying an existing line output as a template.

```{python}
new_output = outputs.OUTPUT_9.copy()
```

Change the output type from integral wave parameters (`type=1`) to spectral (`type=4`), and update the metadata.

```{python}
new_output.title = "Thames Estuary BC"
new_output.file_name = "ThamesEstuary_spectral.dfsu"
new_output.type = 4
new_output.format = 0
new_output.include = 1
```

Define the boundary line across the Thames Estuary — a slanted line from the Essex coast (Foulness Point) to the Kent coast (near Margate).

```{python}
new_output.LINE.x_first = 0.8
new_output.LINE.y_first = 51.65
new_output.LINE.x_last = 1.4
new_output.LINE.y_last = 51.3
new_output.LINE.npoints = 20
```

Visualize the line on the mesh.

```{python}
ax = msh.plot()
line = new_output.LINE
ax.plot(
    [line.x_first, line.x_last],
    [line.y_first, line.y_last],
    "r-",
    linewidth=2,
    label="Nesting boundary",
)
ax.legend();
```

## Update the setup

Add the new output and update the counts.

```{python}
outputs["OUTPUT_11"] = new_output
outputs.number_of_outputs = 11
outputs.MzSEPfsListItemCount = 11
```

Verify by inspecting the updated outputs.

```{python}
df = outputs.to_dataframe(prefix="OUTPUT_")
df[["title", "file_name", "type", "include"]]
```

Write the modified setup to a new file.

```{python}
pfs.write("NorthSea_nested.sw")
```

Verify the round-trip by re-reading.

```{python}
pfs2 = mikeio.read_pfs("NorthSea_nested.sw")
df2 = pfs2.FemEngineSW.SPECTRAL_WAVE_MODULE.OUTPUTS.to_dataframe(prefix="OUTPUT_")
df2[["title", "file_name", "type", "include"]]
```

## Clean up

```{python}
import os

os.remove("NorthSea_nested.sw")
```
